page: UpCloud CLOUDNATIVE-32xCPU-256GB is a memory-optimized virtual server running on the KVM hypervisor. It features 32 vCPUs mapped to 32 physical cores of an AMD EPYC 7542 processor operating at a base frequency of 2.0 GHz. The system is equipped with 256.0 GB of RAM, providing a high memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per core. The CPU architecture is x86_64, utilizing a shared allocation model. It does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware.

Benchmark results indicate a contrast between single-core and multi-core capabilities. The single-core performance is relatively weak, with stress-ng single-core scoring in the bottom 10% at 746.58 and Geekbench single-core scoring 1255.0. However, multi-core and parallel processing metrics are robust. The Passmark CPU score reaches 47534.81, placing it in the top 25%, while multi-threaded bzip3 decompression performs strongly at 242.89 GB/sec. Memory bandwidth is stable, averaging 99.45 GB/sec for large block reads and 44.93 GB/sec for large block writes.

From a resource density perspective, this server offers high memory capacity and core density, making it qualitatively cost-efficient for workloads that scale horizontally across many threads rather than relying on high single-thread clock speeds. The primary tradeoff is the shared CPU allocation and the low 2.0 GHz base clock speed, which limits its suitability for latency-sensitive, single-threaded applications. The absence of bundled local storage requires external block storage attachments.

This configuration is well-suited for memory-intensive, multi-threaded workloads. Appropriate use cases include large-scale database hosting, in-memory caching systems like Redis, multi-threaded data decompression, software compilation, and moderate-scale LLM prompt processing.
description: The UpCloud CLOUDNATIVE-32xCPU-256GB is a memory-optimized virtual server powered by an AMD EPYC 7542 processor with 32 vCPUs and 256.0 GB of RAM. Operating on the KVM hypervisor with an x86_64 architecture, this instance provides a high memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per core under a shared CPU allocation model. While single-core performance is limited, with stress-ng single-core scores in the bottom 10%, multi-threaded capabilities are strong, as evidenced by a Passmark CPU score of 47534.81 and high multi-threaded decompression speeds. The server lacks local storage and dedicated GPUs, making it a specialized choice for memory-heavy, parallelized tasks. It is highly suited for large database operations, in-memory caching, software compilation, and multi-threaded data processing workloads.
og_description: UpCloud CLOUDNATIVE-32xCPU-256GB is a memory-optimized KVM virtual server featuring 32 AMD EPYC vCPUs and 256 GB of RAM, designed for highly parallelized, memory-intensive workloads.

tagline: A high-density, memory-optimized virtual server featuring 32 cores and 256 GB RAM for demanding multi-threaded database and caching workloads.

High memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per core

Shared CPU allocation on KVM hypervisor
